description: Dub is the modern link attribution platform for short links, conversion tracking, and affiliate programs.

tools.name: bounties_submissions_approve-bounty
tools.description: Approve a bounty submission. Optionally specify a custom reward amount. Required: bountyId, submissionId. Optional: rewardAmount. Returns the new ApproveBountyResponse.

params.name: bountyId
params.description: The ID of the bounty

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The ID of the bounty submission

params.name: rewardAmount
params.description: The reward amount for the performance-based bounty. Applicable if the bounty reward amount is not set.

tools.name: bounties_submissions_list-bounty
tools.description: List all submissions for a specific bounty in your partner program. Required: bountyId. Optional: status, groupId, partnerId (plus 4 more). Returns array of ListBountyItem.

params.name: bountyId
params.description: The unique ID of the bounty on Dub. Can be found in the URL of the bounty page, prefixed with `bnty_`.

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the submissions to list.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The ID of the group to list submissions for.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to list submissions for.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the submissions by.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The order to sort the submissions by.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: bounties_submissions_reject-bounty
tools.description: Reject a bounty submission with a specified reason and optional note. Required: bountyId, submissionId. Optional: rejectionNote, rejectionReason. Returns the new RejectBountyResponse.

params.name: bountyId
params.description: The ID of the bounty

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The ID of the bounty submission

params.name: rejectionNote
params.description: The note for rejecting the submission.

params.name: rejectionReason
params.description: The reason for rejecting the submission.

tools.name: commissions_bulk-update
tools.description: Bulk update up to 100 commissions with the same status. Required: commissionIds, status. Returns array of BulkUpdateItem.

params.name: commissionIds
params.description: Commission ids

params.name: status
params.description: The status to apply to every commission in the batch.

tools.name: commissions_list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of commissions for your partner program. Optional: type, customerId, payoutId (plus 15 more). Returns array of ListItem.

params.name: type
params.description: Type

params.name: customerId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated customer.

params.name: payoutId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated payout.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated partner. When specified, takes precedence over `tenantId`.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated partner's `tenantId` (their unique ID within your database).

params.name: groupId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated partner group.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by the associated invoice. Since invoiceId is unique on a per-program basis, this will only return one commission per invoice.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter the list of commissions by their corresponding status.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the list of commissions by.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order for the list of commissions.

params.name: interval
params.description: The interval to retrieve commissions for.

params.name: start
params.description: The start date of the date range to filter the commissions by.

params.name: end
params.description: The end date of the date range to filter the commissions by.

params.name: timezone
params.description: Timezone

params.name: endingBefore
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `startingAfter`.

params.name: startingAfter
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `endingBefore`.

params.name: page
params.description: DEPRECATED. Use `startingAfter` instead.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: commissions_update
tools.description: Update an existing commission amount. This is useful for handling refunds (partial or full) or fraudulent sales. Required: id. Optional: amount, currency (default: usd), earnings (plus 4 more). Returns the updated UpdateResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: The commission's unique ID on Dub.

params.name: amount
params.description: Deprecated. Use `saleAmount` instead.

params.name: currency
params.description: The currency of the sale amount to update. Accepts ISO 4217 currency codes.

params.name: earnings
params.description: The new earnings amount for the commission. Paid commissions cannot be updated. If provided, will override the earnings calculated based on the sale amount and currency.

params.name: modifyAmount
params.description: Deprecated. Use `modifySaleAmount` instead.

params.name: modifySaleAmount
params.description: Modify the current sale amount: use positive values to increase the amount, negative values to decrease it. Takes precedence over `saleAmount`. Paid commissions cannot be updated.

params.name: saleAmount
params.description: The new absolute amount for the sale. Paid commissions cannot be updated.

params.name: status
params.description: Useful for marking a commission as pending, refunded, duplicate, canceled, or fraudulent. Takes precedence over `saleAmount` and `modifySaleAmount`. When a commission is marked as pending, refunded, duplicate, canceled, or fraudulent, it will be omitted from the payout, and the payout amount will be recalculated accordingly. Paid commissions cannot be updated.

tools.name: customers_delete
tools.description: Delete a customer from a workspace. Required: id. Returns the DeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: The unique ID of the customer. You may use either the customer's `id` on Dub (obtained via `/customers` endpoint) or their `externalId` (unique ID within your system, prefixed with `ext_`, e.g. `ext_123`).

tools.name: customers_get
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of customers for the authenticated workspace. Optional: email, externalId, search (plus 11 more). Returns array of GetItem.

params.name: email
params.description: A case-sensitive filter on the list based on the customer's `email` field. The value must be a string. Takes precedence over `externalId`.

params.name: externalId
params.description: A case-sensitive filter on the list based on the customer's `externalId` field. The value must be a string. Takes precedence over `search`.

params.name: search
params.description: A search query to filter customers by email, externalId, or name. If `email` or `externalId` is provided, this will be ignored.

params.name: country
params.description: A filter on the list based on the customer's `country` field.

params.name: linkId
params.description: A filter on the list based on the customer's `linkId` field (the referral link ID).

params.name: programId
params.description: Program ID to filter by.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: Partner ID to filter by.

params.name: includeExpandedFields
params.description: Whether to include expanded fields on the customer (`link`, `partner`, `discount`).

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the customers by. The default is `createdAt`.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order. The default is `desc`.

params.name: endingBefore
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `startingAfter`.

params.name: startingAfter
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `endingBefore`.

params.name: page
params.description: DEPRECATED. Use `startingAfter` instead.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: customers_get-id
tools.description: Retrieve a customer by ID for the authenticated workspace. To retrieve a customer by external ID, prefix the ID with `ext_`. Required: id. Optional: includeExpandedFields. Returns the GetIdResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: The unique ID of the customer. You may use either the customer's `id` on Dub (obtained via `/customers` endpoint) or their `externalId` (unique ID within your system, prefixed with `ext_`, e.g. `ext_123`).

params.name: includeExpandedFields
params.description: Whether to include expanded fields on the customer (`link`, `partner`, `discount`).

tools.name: customers_update
tools.description: Update a customer for the authenticated workspace. Required: id. Optional: includeExpandedFields, avatar, country (plus 4 more). Returns the updated UpdateResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: The unique ID of the customer. You may use either the customer's `id` on Dub (obtained via `/customers` endpoint) or their `externalId` (unique ID within your system, prefixed with `ext_`, e.g. `ext_123`).

params.name: includeExpandedFields
params.description: Whether to include expanded fields on the customer (`link`, `partner`, `discount`).

params.name: avatar
params.description: The customer's avatar URL. If not provided, a random avatar will be generated.

params.name: country
params.description: The customer's country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format. Updating this field will only affect the customer's country in Dub's system (and has no effect on existing conversion events).

params.name: email
params.description: The customer's email address.

params.name: externalId
params.description: The customer's unique identifier your database. This is useful for associating subsequent conversion events from Dub's API to your internal systems.

params.name: name
params.description: The customer's name. If not provided, the email address will be used, and if email is not provided, a random name will be generated.

params.name: stripeCustomerId
params.description: The customer's Stripe customer ID. This is useful for attributing recurring sale events to the partner who referred the customer.

tools.name: domains_check-status
tools.description: Check if a domain name is available for purchase. You can check multiple domains at once. Required: domains. Returns array of CheckStatusItem.

params.name: domains
params.description: The domains to search. We only support .link domains for now.

tools.name: domains_create
tools.description: Create a domain for the authenticated workspace. Required: slug. Optional: appleAppSiteAssociation, archived (default: false), assetLinks (plus 4 more). Returns the new DomainSchema.

params.name: appleAppSiteAssociation
params.description: apple-app-site-association configuration file (for deep link support on iOS).

params.name: archived
params.description: Whether to archive this domain. `false` will unarchive a previously archived domain.

params.name: assetLinks
params.description: assetLinks.json configuration file (for deep link support on Android).

params.name: expiredUrl
params.description: Redirect users to a specific URL when any link under this domain has expired.

params.name: logo
params.description: The logo of the domain.

params.name: notFoundUrl
params.description: Redirect users to a specific URL when a link under this domain doesn't exist.

params.name: placeholder
params.description: Provide context to your teammates in the link creation modal by showing them an example of a link to be shortened.

params.name: slug
params.description: Name of the domain.

tools.name: domains_delete
tools.description: Delete a domain from a workspace. It cannot be undone. This will also delete all the links associated with the domain. Required: slug. Returns the DeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: slug
params.description: The domain name.

tools.name: domains_list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of domains for the authenticated workspace. Optional: archived (default: false), search, page (plus 1 more). Returns array of DomainSchema.

params.name: archived
params.description: Whether to include archived domains in the response. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: search
params.description: The search term to filter the domains by.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: domains_register
tools.description: Register a domain for the authenticated workspace. Only available for Enterprise Plans. Required: domain. Returns the new RegisterResponse.

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain to claim. We only support .link domains for now.

tools.name: domains_update
tools.description: Update a domain for the authenticated workspace. Required: slug. Optional: appleAppSiteAssociation, archived (default: false), assetLinks (plus 4 more). Returns the updated DomainSchema.

params.name: slug
params.description: The domain name.

params.name: appleAppSiteAssociation
params.description: apple-app-site-association configuration file (for deep link support on iOS).

params.name: archived
params.description: Whether to archive this domain. `false` will unarchive a previously archived domain.

params.name: assetLinks
params.description: assetLinks.json configuration file (for deep link support on Android).

params.name: expiredUrl
params.description: Redirect users to a specific URL when any link under this domain has expired.

params.name: logo
params.description: The logo of the domain.

params.name: notFoundUrl
params.description: Redirect users to a specific URL when a link under this domain doesn't exist.

params.name: placeholder
params.description: Provide context to your teammates in the link creation modal by showing them an example of a link to be shortened.

tools.name: dub-analytics_retrieve-analytics
tools.description: Retrieve analytics for a link, a domain, or the authenticated workspace. The response type depends on the `event` and `type` query parameters. Optional: event (default: clicks), groupBy (default: count), domain (plus 35 more).

params.name: event
params.description: The type of event to retrieve analytics for. Defaults to `clicks`.

params.name: groupBy
params.description: The parameter to group the analytics data points by. Defaults to `count` if undefined.

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain to filter analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `dub.co`, `dub.co,google.com`, `-spam.com`.

params.name: key
params.description: The slug of the short link to retrieve analytics for. Must be used along with the corresponding `domain` of the short link to fetch analytics for a specific short link.

params.name: linkId
params.description: The unique ID of the link to retrieve analytics for.Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `link_123`, `link_123,link_456`, `-link_789`.

params.name: externalId
params.description: The ID of the link in the your database. Must be prefixed with 'ext_' when passed as a query parameter.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the tenant that created the link inside your system. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `tenant_123`, `tenant_123,tenant_456`, `-tenant_789`.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The tag ID to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `tag_123`, `tag_123,tag_456`, `-tag_789`.

params.name: folderId
params.description: The folder ID to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `folder_123`, `folder_123,folder_456`, `-folder_789`. If not provided, return analytics for all links.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The group ID to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `grp_123`, `grp_123,grp_456`, `-grp_789`.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `pn_123`, `pn_123,pn_456`, `-pn_789`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The ID of the customer to retrieve analytics for.

params.name: interval
params.description: The interval to retrieve analytics for. If undefined, defaults to 24h.

params.name: start
params.description: The start date and time when to retrieve analytics from. If set, takes precedence over `interval`.

params.name: end
params.description: The end date and time when to retrieve analytics from. If not provided, defaults to the current date. If set along with `start`, takes precedence over `interval`.

params.name: timezone
params.description: The IANA time zone code for aligning timeseries granularity (e.g. America/New_York). Defaults to UTC.

params.name: country
params.description: The country to retrieve analytics for. Must be passed as a 2-letter ISO 3166-1 country code (see https://d.to/geo). Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `US`, `US,BR,FR`, `-US`.

params.name: city
params.description: The city to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `New York`, `New York,London`, `-New York`.

params.name: region
params.description: The ISO 3166-2 region code to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `NY`, `NY,CA`, `-NY`.

params.name: continent
params.description: The continent to retrieve analytics for. Valid values: AF, AN, AS, EU, NA, OC, SA. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `NA`, `NA,EU`, `-AS`.

params.name: device
params.description: The device to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `Desktop`, `Mobile,Tablet`, `-Mobile`.

params.name: browser
params.description: The browser to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `Chrome`, `Chrome,Firefox,Safari`, `-IE`.

params.name: os
params.description: The OS to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `Windows`, `Mac,Windows,Linux`, `-Windows`.

params.name: trigger
params.description: The trigger to retrieve analytics for. Valid values: qr, link, pageview. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `qr`, `qr,link`, `-qr`. If undefined, returns all trigger types.

params.name: referer
params.description: The referer hostname to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `google.com`, `google.com,twitter.com`, `-facebook.com`.

params.name: refererUrl
params.description: The full referer URL to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `https://google.com`, `https://google.com,https://twitter.com`, `-https://spam.com`.

params.name: url
params.description: The destination URL to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `https://example.com`, `https://example.com,https://other.com`, `-https://spam.com`.

params.name: utm_source
params.description: The UTM source to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `google`, `google,twitter`, `-spam`.

params.name: utm_medium
params.description: The UTM medium to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `cpc`, `cpc,social`, `-email`.

params.name: utm_campaign
params.description: The UTM campaign to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`). Examples: `summer_sale`, `summer_sale,winter_sale`, `-old_campaign`.

params.name: utm_term
params.description: The UTM term to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`).

params.name: utm_content
params.description: The UTM content to retrieve analytics for. Supports advanced filtering: single value, multiple values (comma-separated), or exclusion (prefix with `-`).

params.name: root
params.description: Filter for root domains. If true, filter for domains only. If false, filter for links only. If undefined, return both.

params.name: saleType
params.description: Filter sales by type: 'new' for first-time purchases, 'recurring' for repeat purchases. If undefined, returns both.

params.name: query
params.description: Search the events by a custom metadata value. Only available for lead and sale events. Examples: `metadata['key']:'value'`

params.name: programId
params.description: Deprecated: This is automatically inferred from your workspace's defaultProgramId. The ID of the program to retrieve analytics for.

params.name: tagIds
params.description: Deprecated: Use `tagId` instead. The tag IDs to retrieve analytics for.

params.name: qr
params.description: Deprecated: Use the `trigger` field instead. Filter for QR code scans. If true, filter for QR codes only. If false, filter for links only. If undefined, return both.

tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of events for the authenticated workspace. Optional: event (default: clicks), domain, key (plus 39 more). Returns array of ListItem.

params.description: The field to sort the events by. The default is `timestamp`.

params.name: order
params.description: DEPRECATED. Use `sortOrder` instead.

tools.name: folders_create
tools.description: Create a folder for the authenticated workspace. Required: name. Optional: accessLevel, description. Returns the new FolderSchema.

params.name: accessLevel
params.description: The access level of the folder within the workspace.

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the folder.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the folder.

tools.name: folders_delete
tools.description: Delete a folder from the workspace. All existing links will still work, but they will no longer be associated with this folder. Required: id. Returns the DeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the folder to delete.

tools.name: folders_list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of folders for the authenticated workspace. Optional: search, page, pageSize (default: 50). Returns array of FolderSchema.

params.name: search
params.description: The search term to filter the folders by.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.description: Update a folder in the workspace. Required: id. Optional: accessLevel, description, name. Returns the updated FolderSchema.

params.name: id

params.name: accessLevel
params.description: The access level of the folder within the workspace.

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the folder.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the folder.

tools.name: links_bulk-create
tools.description: Bulk create up to 100 links for the authenticated workspace. Returns array of BulkCreateItem.

tools.name: links_bulk-delete
tools.description: Bulk delete up to 100 links for the authenticated workspace. Required: linkIds. Returns the BulkDeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: linkIds
params.description: Comma-separated list of link IDs to delete. Maximum of 100 IDs. Non-existing IDs will be ignored.

tools.name: links_bulk-update
tools.description: Bulk update up to 100 links with the same data for the authenticated workspace. Optional: externalIds (default: []), linkIds (default: []). Returns array of LinkSchema.

params.name: externalIds
params.description: The external IDs of the links to update as stored in your database.

params.name: linkIds
params.description: The IDs of the links to update. Takes precedence over `externalIds`.

tools.name: links_create
tools.description: Create a link for the authenticated workspace. Required: url. Optional: android, archived, comments (plus 34 more). Returns the new LinkSchema.

params.name: android
params.description: The Android destination URL for the short link for Android device targeting.

params.name: archived
params.description: Whether the short link is archived. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: comments
params.description: The comments for the short link.

params.name: description
params.description: The custom link preview description (og:description). Will be used for Custom Link Previews if `proxy` is true. Learn more: https://d.to/og

params.name: doIndex
params.description: Allow search engines to index your short link. Defaults to `false` if not provided. Learn more: https://d.to/noindex

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain of the short link (without protocol). If not provided, the primary domain for the workspace will be used (or `dub.sh` if the workspace has no domains).

params.name: expiredUrl
params.description: The URL to redirect to when the short link has expired.

params.name: expiresAt
params.description: The date and time when the short link will expire at.

params.name: externalId
params.description: The ID of the link in your database. If set, it can be used to identify the link in future API requests (must be prefixed with 'ext_' when passed as a query parameter). This key is unique across your workspace.

params.name: folderId
params.description: The unique ID existing folder to assign the short link to.

params.name: image
params.description: The custom link preview image (og:image). Will be used for Custom Link Previews if `proxy` is true. Learn more: https://d.to/og

params.name: ios
params.description: The iOS destination URL for the short link for iOS device targeting.

params.name: key
params.description: The short link slug. If not provided, a random 7-character slug will be generated.

params.name: keyLength
params.description: The length of the short link slug. Defaults to 7 if not provided. When used with `prefix`, the total length of the key will be `prefix.length + keyLength`.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner the short link is associated with.

params.name: password
params.description: The password required to access the destination URL of the short link.

params.name: prefix
params.description: The prefix of the short link slug for randomly-generated keys (e.g. if prefix is `/c/`, generated keys will be in the `/c/:key` format). Will be ignored if `key` is provided.

params.name: programId
params.description: The ID of the program the short link is associated with.

params.name: proxy
params.description: Whether the short link uses Custom Link Previews feature. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: publicStats
params.description: Deprecated: Use `dashboard` instead. Whether the short link's stats are publicly accessible. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: ref
params.description: The referral tag of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the `ref` query parameter in the destination URL.

params.name: rewrite
params.description: Whether the short link uses link cloaking. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: tagId
params.description: Deprecated: Use `tagIds` instead. The unique ID of the tag assigned to the short link.

params.name: tagIds
params.description: The unique IDs of the tags assigned to the short link.

params.name: tagNames
params.description: The unique name of the tags assigned to the short link (case insensitive).

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the tenant that created the link inside your system. If set, it can be used to fetch all links for a tenant.

params.name: testCompletedAt
params.description: The date and time when the tests were or will be completed.

params.name: testStartedAt
params.description: The date and time when the tests started.

params.name: title
params.description: The custom link preview title (og:title). Will be used for Custom Link Previews if `proxy` is true. Learn more: https://d.to/og

params.name: trackConversion
params.description: Whether to track conversions for the short link. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: url
params.description: The destination URL of the short link.

params.name: utm_campaign
params.description: The UTM campaign of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the UTM campaign in the destination URL.

params.name: utm_content
params.description: The UTM content of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the UTM content in the destination URL.

params.name: utm_medium
params.description: The UTM medium of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the UTM medium in the destination URL.

params.name: utm_source
params.description: The UTM source of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the UTM source in the destination URL.

params.name: utm_term
params.description: The UTM term of the short link. If set, this will populate or override the UTM term in the destination URL.

params.name: video
params.description: The custom link preview video (og:video). Will be used for Custom Link Previews if `proxy` is true. Learn more: https://d.to/og

params.name: webhookIds
params.description: An array of webhook IDs to trigger when the link is clicked. These webhooks will receive click event data.

tools.name: links_delete
tools.description: Delete a link for the authenticated workspace. Required: linkId. Returns the DeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: linkId
params.description: The id of the link to delete. You may use either `linkId` (obtained via `/links/info` endpoint) or `externalId` prefixed with `ext_`.

tools.name: links_get
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of links for the authenticated workspace. Optional: domain, tagId, tagIds (plus 14 more). Returns array of LinkSchema.

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain to filter the links by. E.g. `ac.me`. If not provided, all links for the workspace will be returned.

params.name: tagId
params.description: Deprecated: Use `tagIds` instead. The tag ID to filter the links by.

params.name: tagIds
params.description: The tag IDs to filter the links by.

params.name: tagNames
params.description: The unique name of the tags assigned to the short link (case insensitive).

params.name: folderId
params.description: The folder ID to filter the links by.

params.name: search
params.description: The search term to filter the links by. The search term will be matched against the short link slug and the destination url.

params.name: userId
params.description: The user ID to filter the links by.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the tenant that created the link inside your system. If set, will only return links for the specified tenant.

params.name: showArchived
params.description: Whether to include archived links in the response. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: withTags
params.description: DEPRECATED. Filter for links that have at least one tag assigned to them.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the links by. The default is `createdAt`.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order. The default is `desc`.

params.name: sort
params.description: DEPRECATED. Use `sortBy` instead.

params.name: endingBefore
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `startingAfter`.

params.name: startingAfter
params.description: If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. Mutually exclusive with `endingBefore`.

params.name: page
params.description: DEPRECATED. Use `startingAfter` instead.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: links_get-count
tools.description: Retrieve the number of links for the authenticated workspace. Optional: domain, tagId, tagIds (plus 8 more).

params.name: domain
params.description: The domain to filter the links by. E.g. `ac.me`. If not provided, all links for the workspace will be returned.

params.name: tagId
params.description: Deprecated: Use `tagIds` instead. The tag ID to filter the links by.

params.name: tagNames
params.description: The unique name of the tags assigned to the short link (case insensitive).

params.name: folderId
params.description: The folder ID to filter the links by.

params.description: The search term to filter the links by. The search term will be matched against the short link slug and the destination url.

params.description: The user ID to filter the links by.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the tenant that created the link inside your system. If set, will only return links for the specified tenant.

params.name: showArchived
params.description: Whether to include archived links in the response. Defaults to `false` if not provided.

params.name: withTags
params.description: DEPRECATED. Filter for links that have at least one tag assigned to them.

params.name: groupBy
params.description: The field to group the links by.

tools.name: partners_approve
tools.description: Approve a pending partner application to your program. The partner will be enrolled in the specified group and notified of the approval. Required: partnerId. Optional: groupId. Returns the new ApproveResponse.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The ID of the group to assign the partner to. If not provided, the partner will be assigned to the group they applied to, or the program's default group if no application group is set.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to approve.

tools.name: partners_ban
tools.description: Ban a partner from your program. This will disable all links and mark all commissions as canceled. Required: reason. Optional: partnerId, tenantId. Returns the new BanResponse.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: reason
params.description: The reason for banning the partner.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

tools.name: partners_create
tools.description: Creates or updates a partner record (upsert behavior). If a partner with the same email already exists, their program enrollment will be updated with the provided tenantId. If no existing partner is found, a new partner will be created using the supplied information. Required: email. Optional: country, description, groupId (plus 4 more). Returns the new CreateResponse.

params.name: country
params.description: The partner's country of residence. Must be passed as a 2-letter ISO 3166-1 country code. See https://d.to/geo for more information.

params.name: description
params.description: A brief description of the partner and their background. Max 5,000 characters.

params.name: email
params.description: The partner's email address. Partners will be able to claim their profile by signing up at `partners.dub.co` with this email.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The group ID to add the partner to. If not provided, the partner will be added to the default group.

params.name: image
params.description: The partner's avatar image. If not provided, a default avatar will be used.

params.name: name
params.description: The partner's full name. If undefined, the partner's email will be used in lieu of their name (e.g. `john@acme.com`)

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The partner's unique ID in your system. Useful for retrieving the partner's links and stats later on. If not provided, the partner will be created as a standalone partner.

params.name: username
params.description: The partner's unique username in your system (max 100 characters). This will be used to create a short link for the partner using your program's default domain. If not provided, Dub will try to generate a username from the partner's name or email.

tools.name: partners_create-link
tools.description: Create a link for a partner that is enrolled in your program. Optional: comments, key, partnerId (plus 2 more). Returns the new LinkSchema.

params.name: comments
params.description: The comments for the short link.

params.name: key
params.description: The short link slug. If not provided, a random 7-character slug will be generated.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to shorten (if not provided, the program's default URL will be used). Will throw an error if the domain doesn't match the program's default URL domain.

tools.name: partners_deactivate
tools.description: This will deactivate the partner from your program and disable all their active links. Their commissions and payouts will remain intact. You can reactivate them later if needed. Optional: partnerId, tenantId. Returns the new DeactivateResponse.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

tools.name: partners_list
tools.description: List all partners for a partner program. Optional: groupId, status, country (plus 7 more). Returns array of ListItem.

params.name: groupId
params.description: A filter on the list based on the partner's `groupId` field.

params.name: status
params.description: A filter on the list based on the partner's `status` field.

params.name: country
params.description: A filter on the list based on the partner's `country` field.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the partners by. The default is `totalSaleAmount`.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order. The default is `desc`.

params.name: email
params.description: Filter the partner list based on the partner's `email`. The value must be a string. Takes precedence over `search`.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: Filter the partner list based on the partner's `tenantId`. The value must be a string. Takes precedence over `email` and `search`.

params.name: search
params.description: A search query to filter partners by ID, name, email, or link.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: partners_list-applications
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of pending applications for your partner program. Optional: country, groupId, page (plus 1 more). Returns array of ListApplicationsItem.

params.name: country
params.description: A filter on the list based on the partner's `country` field.

params.name: groupId
params.description: A filter on the list based on the partner's `groupId` field.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: partners_reject
tools.description: Reject a pending partner application to your program. The partner will be notified via email that their application was not approved. Required: partnerId. Optional: allowImmediateReapply (default: false), rejectionNote, rejectionReason. Returns the new RejectResponse.

params.name: allowImmediateReapply
params.description: When true, pending enrollment is removed so the partner can submit a new application immediately.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to reject.

params.name: rejectionNote
params.description: Additional details about the rejection. This will be shared with the partner via email.

params.name: rejectionReason
params.description: The reason for rejecting the partner application. This will be shared with the partner via email.

tools.name: partners_retrieve-analytics
tools.description: Retrieve analytics for a partner within a program. The response type vary based on the `groupBy` query parameter. Optional: partnerId, tenantId, interval (plus 5 more).

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

params.name: interval
params.description: The interval to retrieve analytics for. If undefined, defaults to 24h.

params.name: start
params.description: The start date and time when to retrieve analytics from. If set, takes precedence over `interval`.

params.name: end
params.description: The end date and time when to retrieve analytics from. If not provided, defaults to the current date. If set along with `start`, takes precedence over `interval`.

params.name: timezone
params.description: The IANA time zone code for aligning timeseries granularity (e.g. America/New_York). Defaults to UTC.

params.name: query
params.description: Search the events by a custom metadata value. Only available for lead and sale events. Examples: `metadata['key']:'value'`

params.name: groupBy
params.description: The parameter to group the analytics data points by. Defaults to `count` if undefined.

tools.name: partners_retrieve-links
tools.description: Retrieve a partner's links by their partner ID or tenant ID. Optional: partnerId, tenantId. Returns array of RetrieveLinksItem.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

tools.name: partners_upsert-link
tools.description: Upsert a link for a partner that is enrolled in your program. If a link with the same URL already exists, return it (or update it if there are any changes). Otherwise, a new link will be created. Required: url. Optional: comments, key, partnerId (plus 1 more). Returns the updated LinkSchema.

params.name: comments
params.description: The comments for the short link.

params.name: key
params.description: The short link slug. If not provided, a random 7-character slug will be generated.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: The ID of the partner to create a link for. Will take precedence over `tenantId` if provided.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The ID of the partner in your system. If both `partnerId` and `tenantId` are not provided, an error will be thrown.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to upsert for. Will throw an error if the domain doesn't match the program's default URL domain.

tools.name: payouts_list
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of payouts for your partner program. Optional: status, partnerId, tenantId (plus 5 more). Returns array of ListItem.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter the list of payouts by their corresponding status.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: Filter the list of payouts by the associated partner. When specified, takes precedence over `tenantId`.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: Filter the list of payouts by the associated partner's `tenantId` (their unique ID within your database).

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: Filter the list of payouts by invoice ID (the unique ID of the invoice you receive for each batch payout you process on Dub). Pending payouts will not have an invoice ID.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the list of payouts by.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The sort order for the list of payouts.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: qr_get-qrcode
tools.description: Retrieve a QR code for a link. Required: url. Optional: logo, size (default: 600), level (default: L) (plus 5 more).

params.name: url
params.description: The URL to generate a QR code for.

params.name: logo
params.description: The logo to include in the QR code. Can only be used with a paid plan on Dub.

params.name: size
params.description: The size of the QR code in pixels. Defaults to `600` if not provided.

params.name: level
params.description: The level of error correction to use for the QR code. Defaults to `L` if not provided.

params.name: fgColor
params.description: The foreground color of the QR code in hex format. Defaults to `#000000` if not provided.

params.name: bgColor
params.description: The background color of the QR code in hex format. Defaults to `#ffffff` if not provided.

params.name: hideLogo
params.description: Whether to hide the logo in the QR code. Can only be used with a paid plan on Dub.

params.name: margin
params.description: The size of the margin around the QR code. Defaults to 2 if not provided.

params.name: includeMargin
params.description: DEPRECATED: Margin is included by default. Use the `margin` prop to customize the margin size.

tools.name: tags_create
tools.description: Create a tag for the authenticated workspace. Optional: color, name, tag. Returns the new LinkTagSchemaOutput.

params.name: color
params.description: The color of the tag. If not provided, a random color will be used from the list: red, yellow, green, blue, purple, brown, gray.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the tag to create.

params.name: tag
params.description: The name of the tag to create.

tools.name: tags_delete
tools.description: Delete a tag from the workspace. All existing links will still work, but they will no longer be associated with this tag. Required: id. Returns the DeleteResponse. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the tag to delete.

tools.name: tags_get
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tags for the authenticated workspace. Optional: sortBy (default: name), sortOrder (default: asc), search (plus 3 more). Returns array of LinkTagSchemaOutput.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: The field to sort the tags by.

params.name: sortOrder
params.description: The order to sort the tags by.

params.name: search
params.description: The search term to filter the tags by.

params.name: ids
params.description: IDs of tags to filter by.

params.name: page
params.description: The page number for pagination.

params.name: pageSize
params.description: The number of items per page.

tools.name: tags_update
tools.description: Update a tag in the workspace. Required: id. Optional: color, name, tag. Returns the updated LinkTagSchemaOutput.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the tag to update.

params.name: color
params.description: The color of the tag. If not provided, a random color will be used from the list: red, yellow, green, blue, purple, brown, gray.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the tag to create.

params.name: tag
params.description: The name of the tag to create.

tools.name: tokens_create-referrals-embed
tools.description: Create a referrals embed token for the given partner/tenant. The endpoint first attempts to locate an existing enrollment using the provided tenantId. If no enrollment is found, it resolves the partner by email and creates a new enrollment as needed. This results in an upsert-style flow that guarantees a valid enrollment and returns a usable embed token. Optional: partnerId, tenantId.

params.name: partnerId
params.description: Partner id

params.name: tenantId
params.description: Tenant id

tools.name: track_lead
tools.description: Track a lead for a short link. Required: clickId, customerExternalId, eventName. Optional: customerAvatar, customerEmail, customerName (plus 2 more). Returns the new LeadResponse.

params.name: clickId
params.description: The unique ID of the click that the lead conversion event is attributed to. You can read this value from `dub_id` cookie. [For deferred lead tracking]: If an empty string is provided, Dub will try to find an existing customer with the provided `customerExternalId` and use the `clickId` from the customer if found.

params.name: customerAvatar
params.description: The avatar URL of the customer.

params.name: customerEmail
params.description: The email address of the customer.

params.name: customerExternalId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer in your system. Will be used to identify and attribute all future events to this customer.

params.name: customerName
params.description: The name of the customer. If not passed, a random name will be generated (e.g. “Big Red Caribou”).

params.name: eventName
params.description: The name of the lead event to track. Can also be used as a unique identifier to associate a given lead event for a customer for a subsequent sale event (via the `leadEventName` prop in `/track/sale`).

params.name: eventQuantity
params.description: The numerical value associated with this lead event (e.g., number of provisioned seats in a free trial). If defined as N, the lead event will be tracked N times.

params.name: mode
params.description: The mode to use for tracking the lead event. `async` will not block the request; `wait` will block the request until the lead event is fully recorded in Dub; `deferred` will defer the lead event creation to a subsequent request.

tools.name: track_open
tools.description: This endpoint is used to track when a user opens your app via a Dub-powered deep link (for both iOS and Android). Optional: deepLink, dubDomain. Returns the new OpenResponse.

params.name: deepLink
params.description: The deep link that brought the user to the app. If left blank, Dub will fallback to probabilistic tracking by using the `dubDomain` parameter to check if there is an associated click event for the user's IP address. Learn more: https://d.to/ddl

params.name: dubDomain
params.description: Your deep link custom domain on Dub (e.g. `acme.link`). This is used in probabilistic tracking to check if there is an associated click event for the user's IP address. Learn more: https://d.to/ddl

tools.name: track_sale
tools.description: Track a sale for a short link. Required: amount, customerExternalId. Optional: clickId, currency (default: usd), customerAvatar (plus 6 more). Returns the new SaleResponse.

params.name: amount
params.description: The amount of the sale in cents (for all two-decimal currencies). If the sale is in a zero-decimal currency, pass the full integer value (e.g. `1580` JPY). Learn more: https://d.to/currency

params.name: clickId
params.description: [For direct sale tracking]: The unique ID of the click that the sale conversion event is attributed to. You can read this value from `dub_id` cookie.

params.name: currency
params.description: The currency of the sale. Accepts ISO 4217 currency codes. Sales will be automatically converted and stored as USD at the latest exchange rates. Learn more: https://d.to/currency

params.name: customerAvatar
params.description: [For direct sale tracking]: The avatar URL of the customer.

params.name: customerEmail
params.description: [For direct sale tracking]: The email address of the customer.

params.name: customerExternalId
params.description: The unique ID of the customer in your system. Will be used to identify and attribute all future events to this customer.

params.name: customerName
params.description: [For direct sale tracking]: The name of the customer. If not passed, a random name will be generated (e.g. “Big Red Caribou”).

params.name: eventName
params.description: The name of the sale event. Recommended format: `Invoice paid` or `Subscription created`.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The invoice ID of the sale. Can be used as a idempotency key – only one sale event can be recorded for a given invoice ID.

params.name: leadEventName
params.description: The name of the lead event that occurred before the sale (case-sensitive). This is used to associate the sale event with a particular lead event (instead of the latest lead event for a link-customer combination, which is the default behavior). For direct sale tracking, this field can also be used to specify the lead event name.

params.name: paymentProcessor
params.description: The payment processor via which the sale was made.
